perturbation.original_text: The Information will be kept confidential and shall not without your prior written consent, be disclosed by us, or by our agents, representatives or employees, in any manner whatsover, in whole or in part, and shall not be used by us, our agents, representatives or employees, other than in connection with the transaction described above.
perturbation.changed_text: The Data will be kept confidential and shall not without your prior written consent, be disclosed by us, or by our agents, representatives or employees, in any manner whatsover, in whole or in part, and shall not be used by us, our agents, representatives or employees, other than in connection with the transaction described above.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Information" to "Data" in this section introduces ambiguity. The contract defines "Information" earlier. By using "Data" here, it's unclear if "Data" and "Information" are intended to be synonymous or if "Data" refers to a subset or superset of "Information." This creates an in-text contradiction concerning which information is subject to the confidentiality clause, creating uncertainty and confusion since Data isn't well defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We shall keep a record of the written Information furnished to us and of the location of such Information. All copies of the Information, along with our notes on, summaries and compilations of, or excepts from, the Information, except for that portion of the Information which consists of analyses, compilations, forecasts, studies or other documents prepared by us, our agents, representatives or employees, will be returned to you immediately upon your request. That portion of the Information which consists of analyses, compilations, forecasts, studies or other documents prepared by us, our agents, representatives or employees, will be held by us and kept confidential and subject to the terms of this Agreement, or destroyed upon your request, and any oral Information will continue to be subject to the terms of this Agreement. Such destruction will be confirmed in writing to you by us and the person or persons who prepared such documents.
perturbation.justification: The original text used 'Information,' which is defined elsewhere in the contract and has specific requirements outlined in the 'Contradicted Text' regarding its record-keeping, return, and destruction. The changed text replaces 'Information' with 'Data,' which is undefined in the contract. The 'Contradicted Text' explicitly refers to 'Information,' so changing 'Information' to 'Data' in the changed text introduces ambiguity. The 'Contradicted Text' specifies what happens to 'Information', and with the introduction of 'Data' it is unclear whether 'Data' is subject to the same requirements, creating a direct conflict and inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: Without your prior written consent, we and our Representatives will not communicate with any person or entity that is a party to any agreement with the Business or any possible transaction between us and the Business involving the Business.
perturbation.changed_text: Without your prior written consent, we and our Representatives will not communicate with any person or entity that is a party to any agreement with the Project or any possible transaction between us and the Business involving the Business.
perturbation.explanation: Substituting "Business" with "Project" introduces conflict since "Business" and "Project" are undefined. If a representative were to contact a party related to the "Business" but not specifically the "Project", whether that is a breach is uncertain. This creates an in-text contradiction since the definition of the scope of agreement and who the parties are restricted from talking to is in contradiction, creating enforcement issues.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Without your prior written consent, we and our Representatives will not communicate with any person or entity that is a party to any agreement with the Project or any possible transaction between us and the Business involving the Business.
perturbation.justification: The perturbation substitutes "Business" with "Project" in the original text, creating a contradiction. The explanation states this introduces conflict because "Business" and "Project" are undefined, making it uncertain whether a representative contacting a party related to the "Business" (but not the "Project") constitutes a breach. This contradicts the contradicted text, which uses both terms and therefore causes a contradiction by potentially limiting the scope of the agreement to the Project alone, while the contradicted text addresses both, so the terms aren't clear.

perturbation.original_text: We acknowledge that you make no express or implied representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Information, and you expressly disclaim any and all liability that may be based on the Information, errors therein or omissions therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: We acknowledge that you make no express or implied representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Documentation, and you expressly disclaim any and all liability that may be based on the Information, errors therein or omissions therefrom.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Information" to "Documentation" makes it unclear if the disclaimer of warranty applies to all 'Information' as defined earlier, or only to something termed 'Documentation.' The term Documentation isn't defiend, creating uncertainty and contradiction in the scope of the warranty disclaimer, therefore its unenforcable since it's unclear the reach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We acknowledge that you make no express or implied representation or warranty as to the accuracy or completeness of the Documentation, and you expressly disclaim any and all liability that may be based on the Information, errors therein or omissions therefrom. We agree that we are not entitled to rely on the accuracy or completeness of the Information and that we shall be entitled to rely solely on the representations and warranties made to us by the Business in any final purchase agreement regarding the acquisition.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Information' to 'Documentation' introduces a misaligned terminology contradiction. The original text disclaims warranties for 'Information,' and the changed text disclaims warranties for 'Documentation.' The 'Contradicted Text' contains both 'Documentation' and 'Information,' creating ambiguity about which term is covered by the warranty disclaimer. If 'Documentation' isn't clearly defined, it is unclear what the warranty disclaimer applies to which breaks compatibility with the 'Contradicted Text' and creates an in-text contradiction based on the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition.

perturbation.original_text: In the event that we or anyone to whom we transmit the Information pursuant to this Agreement becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Information, we will provide you with prompt written notice and oral notice so that you may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event that we or anyone to whom we transmit the Data pursuant to this Agreement becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Information, we will provide you with prompt written notice and oral notice so that you may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Information" to "Data" in the first part of the sentence introduces ambiguity regarding which data/information being transmitted triggers the notification requirement. Since earlier the term 'Data' and 'Information' were changed, creating a potential in-text contradiction. Does the notification requirement apply to all 'Information' transmitted, or only the specific 'Data'? This is further conflicted due to the sentence continuing and reffering to the 'Information'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that we or anyone to whom we transmit the Data pursuant to this Agreement becomes legally compelled to disclose any of the Information, we will provide you with prompt written notice and oral notice so that you may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy and/or waive compliance with the provisions of this Agreement. In the event that such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or that the Business waives compliance with the provisions of this Agreement, we will furnish only that portion of the Information which we are advised by the Business or the Business' attorney we are legally required to furnish and we will exercise the efforts directed by the Business to obtain reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded the Information. All costs for actions taken at the direction of the Business shall be subject to indemnification and reimbursement by the Business.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces "Information" with "Data" in a clause that outlines notification requirements when information is legally compelled to be disclosed. The contradicted text refers to both 'Data' and 'Information' in the same clause. The perturbation explanation highlights that this change creates ambiguity regarding which data/information being transmitted triggers the notification requirement, as the contract uses 'Data' and 'Information' and were changed earlier, leading to a conflicting interpretation. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', which states that using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: We agree that, until the expiration of the term of this Agreement pursuant to Paragraph 10 below, neither we nor any of our affiliates, including any person or entity directly or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controlling us or controlled by or under common control with us, will purchase, offer or agree to purchase any securities or assets of the Business, enter, or agree to enter into any acquisition or other business combination, relating to the Business, or make, or induce any other entity to make or negotiate or otherwise deal with others for a tender or exchange offer of Common Stock of the Business, solicit proxies, votes or consents other than for nonimees selected by the Business' Board of Directors, and proposals recommended by the Business' Board of Directors, or otherwise seek to acquire control of the Business unless such purchase, transaction, offer, agreement or proposal shall have previously been approved by the Board of Directors of the Business.
perturbation.changed_text: We agree that, until the expiration of the term of this Agreement pursuant to Paragraph 10 below, neither we nor any of our affiliates, including any person or entity directly or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controlling us or controlled by or under common control with us, will purchase, offer or agree to purchase any securities or assets of the Venture, enter, or agree to enter into any acquisition or other business combination, relating to the Business, or make, or induce any other entity to make or negotiate or otherwise deal with others for a tender or exchange offer of Common Stock of the Business, solicit proxies, votes or consents other than for nonimees selected by the Business' Board of Directors, and proposals recommended by the Business' Board of Directors, or otherwise seek to acquire control of the Business unless such purchase, transaction, offer, agreement or proposal shall have previously been approved by the Board of Directors of the Business.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Business" to "Venture" in the first instance, and keeping the rest with "Business" creates a conflict: what happens if something relates only to the Business but not the Venture, or vice versa? By using the term 'Venture' and 'Business' in the same paragraph and sentence, that reffers to same concept, creates in-text contradiction about the scope of the restriction, leading to possible unenforceability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: We agree that, until the expiration of the term of this Agreement pursuant to Paragraph 10 below, neither we nor any of our affiliates, including any person or entity directly or indirectly through one or more intermediaries, controlling us or controlled by or under common control with us, will purchase, offer or agree to purchase any securities or assets of the Venture, enter, or agree to enter into any acquisition or other business combination, relating to the Business, or make, or induce any other entity to make or negotiate or otherwise deal with others for a tender or exchange offer of Common Stock of the Business, solicit proxies, votes or consents other than for nonimees selected by the Business' Board of Directors, and proposals recommended by the Business' Board of Directors, or otherwise seek to acquire control of the Business unless such purchase, transaction, offer, agreement or proposal shall have previously been approved by the Board of Directors of the Business.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Business' with 'Venture' in the first instance, while the rest of the paragraph and sentence still refer to 'Business'. This creates a contradiction because it's unclear whether the restrictions apply only to the 'Venture', only to the 'Business', or to both. This misalignment of terminology, as described in the perturbation explanation and defined in the Misaligned Terminology definition, directly contradicts the goal of clarity and creates a strong in-text contradiction and ambiguity regarding the scope of the restriction in the contradicted text.
